concept: Railway / Iron Road / The Machine That Runs Underneath
description: An iron road built for a wheeled machine to run on, carrying many people at once, faster and further than a wagon. Coined for the industrial world a transmutation must sometimes describe honestly, not soften: crowded carriages, hurried commutes, the underground lines beneath a Victorian city. 'Underground' stays a separate modifier (muila phou, below the earth) rather than being folded into the word itself, since a railway need not run underground to be one.
sound_symbolism: 'Nu' opens low and grounded, fitting a road that sometimes runs beneath the earth itself. 'Ra' carries the rolling motion, wheel on rail. 'Ko' closes hard, iron meeting iron, the clipped sound of a mechanism rather than a living thing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mia nurako mua wepu.' (I go by railway.) For the underground line specifically: 'muila phou nurako' (the underground railway). A counted railway may take optional 'themo' when treated as crafted infrastructure; the classifier may be omitted when the network or route does not fit that analysis.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Solarpunk is legible only against what it replaces. Naming the extractive-industrial default without flinching is part of naming the regenerative alternative clearly.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Named honestly rather than avoided: a language of cyclical, embodied living still needs the word for the machine that first pulled people away from both, if it is ever going to describe the pulling back.

semantic_domains.creation: The iron road, before the turning
